L1.area: HTTP response data processing and internal utility classes

L2.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists in the HTTP request library where a class that stores a callable property can be exploited via PHP object injection. The class does not implement deserialization safeguards, allowing an attacker to craft a serialized payload with an arbitrary callable that gets executed during normal usage, leading to remote code execution.

L3.area: Internal data iteration and filtering utility classes in the HTTP request library
L3.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists in a utility iterator class within the HTTP request library that stores a callable callback property. The class inherits serialization capabilities from its parent without implementing any deserialization restrictions, allowing an attacker to inject a crafted serialized object with a malicious callback that gets executed when the object is iterated, leading to remote code execution.

ground_truth.reason: The FilteredIterator class extends ArrayIterator and stores a callable callback without implementing deserialization safeguards, allowing an attacker to craft a serialized payload with a malicious callback that executes upon iteration, leading to remote code execution.
